description: Opportunity extraction should keep multiple viable candidates distinct when a source mixes strong funding language with adjacent operational noise.

candidates.title: Community solar and workforce microgrant for cooperative food sites
candidates.summary: A climate equity program offers equipment funding and workforce training for food-growing sites that use cooperative ownership models.
candidates.rationale: The source combines explicit funding language, ecological infrastructure, and member ownership in a way that maps cleanly to the coop's capital formation work.
candidates.recommendedNextStep: Compare the grant size and timeline against current rooftop solar plans before drafting a brief.

candidates.title: Site readiness assistance for cooperative garden infrastructure
candidates.summary: The same program also includes technical assistance and planning support that could help the coop qualify for a later capital round.
candidates.rationale: This is a weaker opportunity than the main grant path, but it still creates a plausible next step by reducing project risk and improving readiness.
candidates.recommendedNextStep: Keep it in the watchlist and revisit if the main solar grant needs site-readiness support.
